error.signature: json.decoder.JSONDecodeError when parsing OpenAI function call arguments in streaming mode

verdict.summary: In streaming mode, function/tool call arguments arrive as partial JSON chunks. Each delta.tool_calls[].function.arguments is a string fragment, not valid JSON. You must concatenate all chunks before parsing.

dead_ends.action: Parse delta.tool_calls[0].function.arguments as JSON per chunk
dead_ends.why_fails: Each streaming chunk contains a partial JSON string fragment like '{"na' or 'me": "'. Parsing individual chunks always fails.

dead_ends.action: Wait for finish_reason='function_call' then parse the last chunk
dead_ends.why_fails: The last chunk doesn't contain the full arguments. You need ALL chunks concatenated. finish_reason='tool_calls' signals completion.

workarounds.action: Accumulate argument string chunks, parse only after finish_reason='tool_calls'
workarounds.how: args_str = ''; for chunk in stream: delta = chunk.choices[0].delta; if delta.tool_calls: args_str += delta.tool_calls[0].function.arguments; # parse after stream ends

workarounds.action: Use non-streaming mode for function calling if latency permits
workarounds.how: response = client.chat.completions.create(stream=False, tools=tools)  # complete JSON in one response

workarounds.action: Use structured output mode (response_format) instead of function calling for complex schemas
